name: talc
description: Talc serves as an anti-caking and bulking agent, improving flow and texture in powdered food products.
raw_text: Talc (Explained + Products) Products Ingredients Decode INCI Login Register Login Register Products Ingredients Decode INCI Follow us on our new Insta page » Talc What-it-does: abrasive/scrub , absorbent/mattifier Irritancy: 0 Comedogenicity: 1 Official CosIng Information All Functions: abrasive, absorbent, anticaking, bulking, opacifying, skin protecting CAS #: 14807-96-6 | EC #: 238-877-9 Chemical/IUPAC Name: Talc (Mg3H2(Sio3)4) (Ci 77718) Cosmetic Restrictions: III/59 Details Talc is the major component of most powder makeup products (think face powder, eyeshadows, and blushers) that usually contain it up to 70%. Its two winning properties that make it very suitable for this role is its outstanding spreadability for a smooth application and its low covering power, aka translucency to avoid clown-like effects. Chemically speaking, it is a clay mineral (hydrated magnesium silicate) that is mined in several countries. The drawback of mined minerals is potential impurities and the version used in cosmetics has to be white (not gray like cheaper grades), free from asbestos, sterilized and have thin plates for a maximum slip.
